title: I Found the Best Shampoos for Fine Hair That Add Volume, Body, and Shine | Vogue
content: I Found the Best Shampoos for Fine Hair That Add Volume, Body, and Shine | Vogue Skip to main content Your All Access Pass GET THE APP Search Account Menu Menu Menu FASHION RUNWAY SHOPPING BEAUTY CULTURE LIVING VOGUE BUSINESS TEEN VOGUE New Arrivals Summer in the City The Vacation Shop Under $300 Shoes Bags Editor Wish Lists FASHION Expand Celebrity Style Street Style Models Designers Trends Sustainability All Fashion RUNWAY SHOPPING Chevron BEAUTY Expand Celebrity Beauty Hair Makeup Nails Wellness Skin All Beauty CULTURE Expand Opinion TV & Movies Music Arts Books News Sports All Culture LIVING Expand Homes Travel Food Parties Royals All Living WEDDINGS Expand Wedding Inspiration Planning & Advice For Wedding Guests The Bridal Shop All Weddings VOGUE BUSINESS Expand Technology Sustainability Fashion Retail Companies Beauty Events Member Benefits Executive Reports VIDEO PHOTOVOGUE MORE NEWSLETTERS PODCAST MAGAZINE PROJECTS VOGUE ARCHIVE TEEN VOGUE Search Search Contact Us About Vogue Beauty I Found the Best Shampoos for Fine Hair That Add Volume, Body, and Shine By Ellie Davis June 25, 2026 Photo: Adobe Stock Save Story Save this story Save Story Save this story I have been on a lifelong search for the best shampoo for fine hair—it is something that I’ve struggled to find as long as I can remember. The perfect shampoo should leave hair looking fuller and healthier, and yet should not feel heavy or leave locks looking flat or limp. The right formula will offer weightless volume, while strengthening the strands for a long-lasting lift. Is that too much to ask? Well, actually no. Vogue’s Favorite Shampoos for Fine Hair: Best for Daily Washing R+Co Bleu Essential Shampoo Read more $49 AMAZON Best for Bond Repair Olaplex Nº. 4 Fine Bond Maintenance Shampoo Read more $34 AMAZON Best Thickening Shampoo Living Proof Full Shampoo Jump to review $36 AMAZON Best for Bouncy Hair Davines VOLU Shampoo Jump to review $38 AMAZON Best for Soft Hair Leonor Greyl Bain Volumateur Aux Algues Shampoo Jump to review $54 AMAZON My hunt for a strand-boosting shampoo to not only clean my hair, but ensure it is sleek and smooth has brought me to some incredibly effective. These hard-working formulas have transformed the first step in my hair-wash routine, and turned what was once a perplexingly flat mane into perfectly polished tresses. The technology in shampoos has evolved to such a point where there is a wealth of bond-building , volumizing , and botanical-based heroes that are featherlight in their consistency, and yet give hair the fuller appearance and resilience that we so strive for. Best of all, the sensorial aspect makes us relish the bi-weekly refresh, imbuing our strands with delightful fragrance with staying power. “Look for shampoos containing panthenol (vitamin B5), amino acids, hydrolyzed proteins, or biotin,” explains cosmetic surgeon Munir Somji, M.B.B.S., a specialist in male and female hair restoration. “And avoid sulfates, heavy silicones, heavy oils and very rich creams.” Scroll on for the Vogue-approved shampoos for fine hair, tried and tested, as well as the answers to our burning questions from the hair expert to decode what fine hair types should be looking for and avoiding—plus, the all-important question of how frequently to wash our delicate strands. In This Story: AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron What ingredients should I look for in a shampoo for fine hair? Should I avoid silicones in shampoo if I have fine hair? How often should fine hair types wash their hair? Can shampoos for fine hair also help with thinning? Should I use clarifying shampoos if I have fine hair? Does biotin help make fine hair thicker? Are protein-based shampoos good for fine hair? What are the signs a shampoo isn’t suitable for fine hair? Meet the expert Best Thickening Shampoo: Living Proof Full Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Living Proof Full Shampoo $36 AMAZON Why We Love It: It was scent of Living Proof’s Full shampoo that first drew me in, an aroma that the hair-care brand’s fan base will be well familiar with as it is featured across the collection. While infusing the signature scent into the strands, it also calls upon a trademarked technology known as Sili-CLONE. This thickening shampoo works to encourage radiance and shine through a thorough cleansing process, ridding your stressed out locks from the dullness causing build-up. Not to mention, it draws upon amaranth peptides to plump the hair fibers, and marine algae polysaccharides to safeguard against environmental damage. Key Ingredients: Amino cleansing complex, amaranth peptides, marine polysaccharide, phytantriol Best for: Volume Size: 236ml Best for Daily Washing : R+Co Bleu Essential Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist R+Co Bleu Essential Shampoo $49 AMAZON $49 NORDSTROM $49 BLUEMERCURY Why We Love It : Vogue alum and beauty expert Sarah Brown previously cited R+Co Bleu’s Essential Shampoo as a favorite. “I’m not supposed to pick favorites, but literally the only shampoo I care about right now and that I wholeheartedly recommend for tout le monde is R+Co Bleu’s Essential Shampoo,” she said. ““I use it daily (yes, I wash my hair daily) with the attendant Essential Conditioner . This shampoo is exactly what its name implies: that essential, easy, do-it-all staple that is geared across the board toward all hair types (I have fine, wavy, short hair) and delivers glossy, bouncy, hydrated hair that smells subtly great.”  The formula is one of those shampoos formulated for a really great wash—delivering moisture and smoothing without toying too far into volumizing, densifying, or hair growth. Key Ingredients : Coconut oil, red marine algae extract Best For : Quenching dry hair, smoothing, adding shine. Size : 8.5 fl oz Best for Silky Soft Strands: Leonor Greyl Bain Volumateur aux Algues Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Leonor Greyl Bain Volumateur Aux Algues Shampoo $54 AMAZON Why We Love It: Parisian hair-care label Leonor Greyl uses predominantly natural ingredients to formulate its strand-supporting shampoos. Designed to tackle common concerns, the Bain Volumateur aux Algues is made for those with long, thin and limp hair to bring volume and bounce. So nourishing is its combination that the brand suggests you need not follow with conditioner, which can weigh down thinner locks. Hair is left shiny and smooth, with a lasting luxurious scent. So good, I cut the bottle in half at the end to make sure I didn’t miss a single drop. Key Ingredients: Alaria seaweed extract, brown seaweed extract, spiruline extract and wheat protein extract Best For: Luxury experience Size: 200ml Best Volumizing Shampoo: Chāmpo Pitta Volumising Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Champo Pitta Volumizing Shampoo $30 CHAMPO Why We Love It: This shampoo from Chāmpo is part of the same collection as the viral hair growth serum , offering support for fine strands and hair loss. Here, predominantly naturally-derived ingredients from peptides to Haritaki Extract, bring resilience to fine or thinning locks. You really don’t need a huge amount of product either, meaning it lasts an age. The results? Smooth, shiny locks, and minimal flyaways. Key Ingredients: Caffeine, tetrapeptides, Ayurvedic Haritaki Extract Best For: Shine, strengthening Size: 30 ml / 1 fl. oz Best for Bond Repair: Olaplex No.4 Fine Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Olaplex Nº. 4 Fine Bond Maintenance Shampoo $34 AMAZON Why We Love It: Fine hair types like myself may have been tempted by the promise of Olaplex. The brand is one of the bestselling haircare brands on the market for its advanced, patented technology that helps rebuild the bonds broken by the likes of chemical, thermal, and environmental damage. That being said, I would use the original No.4 shampoo, and find my hair didn’t feel that clean or soft after use. Fortunately, it was this formula dedicated to fine hair types to the rescue—delivering the lightweight feel we’re lookign for. Apt to boost hydration and strengthen thin strands, my hair feels fuller and more resilient after each wash. Key Ingredients: Olaplex’s Bond Building Technology, flexible polymers, hyaluronic acid Best For: Bond repair, damaged hair, breakage, split ends Size: 250ml Best for Bouncy Hair: Davines VOLU Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Davines VOLU Shampoo $38 AMAZON Why We Love It: Shedding its IYKYK status, Davines is no longer the best-kept secret from the pros–and it is no surprise why. The Italian family behind the label have created high-performance haircare that is no longer reserved for just the in-salon experience. Its VOLU Shampoo in conditioner is formulated to the needs of fine, flat hair types in need of fullness and density—powered by Italian clementine peel extract that features a volume-boosting protein for bouncy hair. Editor’s Experience : “I have used VOLU for a while now and I keep refilling my supply for its root-boosting volume, even while remaining lightweight in feel. It’s incredibly hydrating and nourishing too.” — Ellie Davis, British Vogue’s senior beauty shopping writer Key Ingredients: Clementine peel extract Best For: Bouncy, voluminous hair Size: 250ml Best for Scalp Health: Kat Burki Bio-Ferment Renewing Shampoo Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Kat Burki Bio-Ferment Renewing Shampoo $73 KAT BURKI Why We Love It: Enriched with a cocktail of buzzy ingredients (from rice water to peptides ), Kat Burki’s offering is science-backed to support scalp health—thus creating an optimal environment for growth and strength. Inside, soy proteins repair and strengthen, copper peptides stimulate keratin production–aka the protein that fortifies and minimizes frizz–and pro-B vitamins amplify fullness. Key Ingredients: Pro-B Vitamin complex, fermented rice water, copper peptides Best For: Healthy scalp Size: 250ml Everything You Need to Know What ingredients should I look for in a shampoo for fine hair? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron Fine hair types have to adhere to a fine balance; we want nourishment but not at the expense of weighing down our precious strands. Dr. Somji recommends “looking for lightweight, volumizing, or strengthening formulas that give body without heaviness.” He spotlights the following: “Humectants and hair-friendly nutrients such as panthenol (vitamin B5), amino acids, hydrolyzed proteins, or biotin can help add strength and some extra fullness. Try to avoid heavy silicones, heavy oils, or very rich creams, as these can weigh fine strands down and make them look flat and limp rather than full. Avoid harsh sulfates too, as these can strip natural oils and leave fine hair brittle. Alongside this, consider improving your scalp and hair follicle health and quality. PRP and polynucleotide treatments can help, as can exosome and stem cell topical treatments.” Should I avoid silicones in shampoo if I have fine hair? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron The expert says to “steer clear of heavy silicone-based shampoos and conditioners if your hair is fine because, whilst silicones provide smoothing and shine, they build up over time and can weigh down delicate strands.” How often should people with fine hair wash our hair? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron “This depends on the person and their lifestyle, as everyone’s hair and scalp are different,” explains Dr. Somji. “It is preferable not to wash your hair every day as this can strip the natural oils from your hair and actually make it oily; however, if your scalp is oily, washing more frequently may be necessary. If it’s dry or normal, many people with fine hair benefit from less frequent washing, as over-washing can strip natural oils and damage delicate hair and compromise follicles.” Can shampoos for fine hair also help with thinning? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron While only remaining on your hair for a very short time, Dr Somji tells us, “Shampoos formulated for fine or thinning hair can help support hair health and add volume, but their effect on real thinning is limited. Most ‘volumizing’ shampoos work by coating the hair shaft or by improving moisture and shaft strength, which makes hair look fuller and less fragile.” That being said, he flags, “If underlying hair loss or thinning is due to hormonal change, genetics, or scalp health issues, shampoo alone is unlikely to reverse it, but using a gentle, supportive formula can still help hair look its best while you seek additional solutions.” Should I use clarifying shampoos if I have fine hair? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron In moderation, stresses the expert. “Gentle clarifying shampoo can be useful occasionally (monthly, or when you use lots of styling products) to remove product build-up or oil that might be weighing hair down.” Yet, he stresses, “Frequent use can strip natural oils and leave fine hair dry, brittle or limp.” Does biotin help make fine hair thicker? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron “Yes, oral biotin or a biotin-enriched shampoo can help hair feel stronger and look fuller, especially if combined with good scalp health, a healthy diet, and appropriate care,” confirms Dr. Somji. “But evaluate if hair thinning or shedding is due to an underlying medical issue.” Are protein-based shampoos good for fine hair? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron Protein-based shampoos are worth looking out for. Dr. Somji reveals, “They can help strengthen the hair shaft, improve elasticity, and slightly boost volume without excessive weight.” Although moderation is emphasized. “It is important not to overuse protein shampoos as they can damage the hair and make it feel stiff or cause buildup. It is important to check that the shampoo claims are substantiated and supported by studies and clinical data.” What are the signs a shampoo isn’t suitable for fine hair? AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron “There are different signs and indications that a shampoo isn’t suitable for fine hair, and it is important to recognize that a shampoo is to clean the hair and scalp,” says Dr. Somji. He highlights the following: “If your hair feels flat, heavy, or limp soon after washing or drying. You notice rapid oiliness, scalp buildup, or lack of volume. Hair becomes brittle, dry, or prone to breakage. Roots go flat quickly after styling, or hair lacks bounce and movement.” The expert states that if you notice any of the above, “It’s worth switching to a lighter, silicone-free, volumizing or strengthening formula.” How We Tested AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron When we test and review a product, we take a holistic approach to deliver well-rounded product recommendations. First, we lean on Vogue ’s vast network of experts—from board-certified dermatologists to celebrity estheticians—to gain professional acumen on the industry’s standout products, ones these specialists would actually use on their clients. We pair their expertise with our editorial best practices to curate the thoughtful edits you read on our site. As it relates to shampoos for dry hair, we selected the best based on the following characteristics: ingredients, lather, weight, texture, packaging, finish, and the hair-care concerns they address. To do this, we paired our own personal tests of each formula with expert guidance and reviewer insights to determine which we would recommend to you. Read more about our testing methodology here . Meet the expert AccordionItemContainerButton LargeChevron Dr Munir Somji is an industry expert in aesthetic medicine, overseeing all medical training at DrMediSpa. He is also an expert in both female and male hair restoration and one of the UK’s leading hair transplant surgeons. title: A Strawberry Full Moon Is Coming—Here’s What It Means for Every Star Sign | Vogue
content: A Strawberry Full Moon Is Coming—Here’s What It Means for Every Star Sign | Vogue Skip to main content Your All Access Pass GET THE APP Search Account Menu Menu Menu FASHION RUNWAY SHOPPING BEAUTY CULTURE LIVING VOGUE BUSINESS TEEN VOGUE FASHION Expand Celebrity Style Street Style Models Designers Trends Sustainability All Fashion RUNWAY SHOPPING Chevron BEAUTY Expand Celebrity Beauty Hair Makeup Nails Wellness Skin All Beauty CULTURE Expand Opinion TV & Movies Music Arts Books News Sports All Culture LIVING Expand Homes Travel Food Parties Royals All Living WEDDINGS Expand Wedding Inspiration Planning & Advice For Wedding Guests The Bridal Shop All Weddings VOGUE BUSINESS Expand Technology Sustainability Fashion Retail Companies Beauty Events Member Benefits Executive Reports VIDEO PHOTOVOGUE MORE NEWSLETTERS PODCAST MAGAZINE PROJECTS VOGUE ARCHIVE TEEN VOGUE Search Search Contact Us About Vogue Living A Strawberry Full Moon Is Coming—Here’s What It Means for Every Star Sign By Lisa Stardust June 26, 2026 Photo: Getty Images Save Story Save this story Save Story Save this story The strawberry full moon in Capricorn rises on June 29 at 7:56 p.m. Eastern Time. Notably, the moon signifies when the fruit is in bloom, conveying virtue and the richness in life. It is a lunation of reflection and sympathy that gives us the opportunity to foster love and nurture our spirits. As the first full moon of the summer, and the last full micromoon of the year, it marks a period when practicality clashes with passion, and impulsivity juxtaposes with caution. The strawberry moon aspects Neptune in Aries, a planet known to be extremely tender, but also deceptive. We will be challenging structures we’ve previously built, and trying to comprehend what is tangible and real versus what is based on our illusions. In true Capricorn fashion, we must give our all to what speaks to our hearts. Fernando Salinas, author of The Joy of Tarot, says, “This full moon is all about recognizing that there is a magic to discipline, and that you have to be diligent if you’re going to get the gold medal in the end. I want for you to manifest something huge, something big—think of the mountain the goat of Capricorn has to climb—and then I want you to take one big step towards accomplishing it.” Approximately six and a half hours before the full moon occurs, Mercury also turns retrograde in Cancer. This cosmic back-and-forth is going to cause miscommunications and travel delays, ultimately testing our patience. Be cognizant of the shifts in the sky to avoid dramatic flare-ups with others. And most importantly, we should be gentle with ourselves. Read your strawberry full moon horoscope below: Aries You’ve achieved so much on the professional front, but you never took a moment to honor and appreciate your accomplishments. The strawberry full moon urges you to celebrate yourself by recognizing how far you have come in a short amount of time—and also, what is possible in the foreseeable future. Cheers and toast to your lucrative career—past, present, and future! Taurus What are your dreams, sweet bull? Take stock of your visions to ignite your current aspirations. With the right amount of inspiration and motivation, your intentions can become a reality. The advice here is to believe in yourself completely and to persevere in the face of adversity. With your strength, vigor, and tenacity, there is no doubt you will succeed. Gemini Even though you have the gift of gab, you’re finding that the conversations in several of your relationships are surface-level. Now, you want to get deep and share intimate information with your friends. The first step requires you to set the precedent that the discussions are meant not to judge or criticize, but to be open and clear with them. Cancer Since you give so much of yourself to others, you're focusing on channeling that energy toward healing yourself. Putting their needs on the back burner will feel out of character and leave you unsure how to use your extra time. Hint: A massage or self-care activity is the best bet for realigning and recentering your aura. You deserve it, Cancer! Leo The full moon is one of the moments when you learn you don’t need to have the last word in a dispute; it’s healthier to walk away. On the down low, you enjoy being of service to others, but that is about to evolve into you knowing who and what is noteworthy. You're writing the script as you go. Virgo A humanitarian project ignites your soul with fervor, prompting you to partake in the enriching endeavor. Being part of something larger than yourself will calm your nerves because it helps you get out of your head and introduces you to people with whom you can build lasting relationships. Plus, you are donating your time to enhancing the world with excellence. Libra You are thinking of cutting the cord on situations and partnerships that aren't enlightening your world. Releasing traits, people, and circumstances is proving to be complex because you’re optimistic that affairs will change. If you are not seeing progress, then the odds of it coming to fruition later are slim, which is why you should close this chapter temporarily. Scorpio You’re busier than ever due to the assignments you've recently picked up at work; however, it doesn't excuse you from being of service to a neighbor who requires your assistance. The results of your compassion will lead to a friendlier approach to the community. Pretty soon, you’ll become the person everyone in your neighborhood leans on for favors and support. Sagittarius You never stay in a comfort zone, but this lunation is making you want to seek refuge in what brings you solace. Tuning out the outside noise and pressure you're under is essential in cultivating the relaxing vibe you are bringing into your atmosphere. You are not being selfish by prioritizing your peace and staying true to yourself. Capricorn The full moon is boosting your self-assurance. Your sensitivities are creeping up and peaking, pushing you to embrace and own your sentiments. In times like these, you tend to hide away to avoid confrontation. Rather than repressing your emotions, it's ideal to express them. Doing so can be a game-changer for your interpersonal dynamics and how you relate to others. Aquarius Forgiveness is easy, but forgetting is hard (most struggle with this). The full moon suggests that you let bygones be bygones with those who have formerly wronged you. The caveat is that they wish to jump back into the relationship as if nothing had ever happened. You should take a moment to understand your emotions to navigate the journey forward. Pisces It is time to check in with your values and the importance of keeping good company. While you are firmly planting your feet on a moral high ground, some of the people in your orbit might not be in alignment with yours. In this case, you should try to surround yourself with those who impart similar hopes and altruistic desires. content: 5 Ways to Style A ’60s Shift Dress for Summer | Vogue Skip to main content Your All Access Pass GET THE APP Search Account Menu Menu Menu FASHION RUNWAY SHOPPING BEAUTY CULTURE LIVING VOGUE BUSINESS TEEN VOGUE New Arrivals Summer in the City The Vacation Shop Under $300 Shoes Bags Editor Wish Lists FASHION Expand Celebrity Style Street Style Models Designers Trends Sustainability All Fashion RUNWAY SHOPPING Chevron BEAUTY Expand Celebrity Beauty Hair Makeup Nails Wellness Skin All Beauty CULTURE Expand Opinion TV & Movies Music Arts Books News Sports All Culture LIVING Expand Homes Travel Food Parties Royals All Living WEDDINGS Expand Wedding Inspiration Planning & Advice For Wedding Guests The Bridal Shop All Weddings VOGUE BUSINESS Expand Technology Sustainability Fashion Retail Companies Beauty Events Member Benefits Executive Reports VIDEO PHOTOVOGUE MORE NEWSLETTERS PODCAST MAGAZINE PROJECTS VOGUE ARCHIVE TEEN VOGUE Search Search Contact Us About Vogue Shopping 5 Ways to Style A ’60s Shift Dress for Summer By Olivia Allen June 23, 2026 305pics/Getty Images Save Story Save this story Save Story Save this story Unless you’ve been living under a rock, or have somehow managed to avoid interacting with social media for the past few months, you’ll know we’re in the midst of a full-blown ’90s resurgence . Whether it’s everyone and their mother dying their hair Bessette blonde and donning a pair of Selima Optique glasses, or Rachel Zoe tantalizing us with stories of everyone “in leather jackets and Calvin slip dresses” at Moomba and Bungalow, the not-so-distant decade still has us firmly in its clutches. Vogue ’s Favorite Shift Dresses: The Airy Cotton Mini Posse Danielle dress Jump to review $360 SHOPBOP The Summer Print Dôen Maltina mini dress Jump to review $300 NET-A-PORTER The Pop of Red Zara mini dress Jump to review $70 ZARA The Classic Black Kallmeyer Eloise mini dress Jump to review $695 NET-A-PORTER The Beach-Ready Cap d'Antibes Manon mini dress Jump to review $340 NET-A-PORTER The Vacation Must Faithfull Alondra mini dress Jump to review $270 NET-A-PORTER But change is in the air. Look around on your daily commute and you’ll notice another era making its return: the swinging ’60s–which, funnily enough, inspired all the short skirts, skinny trousers, long blazers and mini dresses of the 1990s–slowly creeping into our wardrobes via shift dresses and Jane Birkin-approved ballet flats galore. Geometric prints are everywhere, The Beatles are once again a cultural talking point, and as the warmer months roll around, we’re looking to the era for fresh inspiration. The clearest sign of all? The street-style set has adopted the classic shift dress as its unofficial uniform for summer. Here’s a handful of ways to style yours. Light and Breezy One of the many virtues of the ’60s shift is its simplicity. Requiring minimal fabric and minimal fuss, it’s ideal for navigating heatwaves and sticky city streets. In cotton or linen, the mini will keep you cool while still looking polished. Pair it with heeled ballet flats and a raffia tote for maximum summer nonchalance. title: The Best Way to Use Biotin For Hair | Vogue
content: The Best Way to Use Biotin For Hair | Vogue Skip to main content Your All Access Pass GET THE APP Search Account Menu Menu Menu FASHION RUNWAY SHOPPING BEAUTY CULTURE LIVING VOGUE BUSINESS TEEN VOGUE FASHION Expand Celebrity Style Street Style Models Designers Trends Sustainability All Fashion RUNWAY SHOPPING Chevron BEAUTY Expand Celebrity Beauty Hair Makeup Nails Wellness Skin All Beauty CULTURE Expand Opinion TV & Movies Music Arts Books News Sports All Culture LIVING Expand Homes Travel Food Parties Royals All Living WEDDINGS Expand Wedding Inspiration Planning & Advice For Wedding Guests The Bridal Shop All Weddings VOGUE BUSINESS Expand Technology Sustainability Fashion Retail Companies Beauty Events Member Benefits Executive Reports VIDEO PHOTOVOGUE MORE NEWSLETTERS PODCAST MAGAZINE PROJECTS VOGUE ARCHIVE TEEN VOGUE Search Search Contact Us About Vogue Shopping The Best Way to Use Biotin For Hair By Marie Courtois , Christina Pérez , and Margaux Anbouba June 26, 2026 Cleo Sullivan / Gallery Stock Save Story Save this story Save Story Save this story Fun, totally not stressful fact: we typically lose up to 50 hairs per day. While this is normally nothing to worry about, you may have noticed that the volume of hair loss changes with the different seasons. This so-called “ seasonal alopecia ” is caused largely by the ebb and flow of temperature, sunlight, and more. Luckily, adopting good hair habits will keep hair looking as healthy and full as possible—regardless of the inevitable seasonal shed. Some ways to do this include drying your hair with a microfiber towel, using a boar bristle brush , and opting for hair products with gentle, natural ingredients. And then there’s biotin, one of the most popular supplements for hair growth and retention. The benefits of biotin for hair Biotin—which gets its name from the Greek prefix bio , meaning “life”—helps the body make energy from food. Like other B vitamins, it’s essential for healthy skin, nails, and hair. Some studies suggest that biotin may be helpful for stimulating hair growth and preventing hair loss. You can get biotin through eating a diverse diet—it’s naturally found in bananas, sweet potatoes, egg yolks, avocado—and it comes in supplement form as well. Dr. Hadley King previously told Vogue that biotin is associated with hair growth because studies have shown that one of the main symptoms of a biotin deficiency is hair loss. But, Dr. King cautions, the relationship between hair loss and biotin hasn’t been studied enough to know for sure. “Additional research is needed to determine whether biotin supplements can improve skin, hair, and nail health in adults,” says Dr. King. “[Especially for those] who aren’t deficient in this vitamin.” How to use biotin for hair loss While scientific evidence is limited, many people anecdotally swear by biotin supplements. Experts share that the vitamin is water-soluble, which means it’s relatively safe to take as a supplement. However, it’s important to note that most experts also agree that additional research on biotin’s true efficacy for hair loss and hair growth is still needed. With that in mind, there are things to consider if you plan to take a biotin supplement —or any over-the-counter supplement, for that matter. Be sure to look for supplements that are third-party lab tested to ensure high quality. And do your due diligence by doing a quick search for other potential risks. For example, a few years ago, the FDA released a warning that taking biotin supplements can interfere with certain lab tests. The daily recommended dose for biotin ranges from 5 to 35 micrograms, but “many dietary supplements promoted for hair, skin, and nail benefits contain biotin levels up to 650 times the recommended daily intake of biotin,” the agency reported . “Biotin in blood or other samples taken from patients who are ingesting high levels of biotin in dietary supplements can cause clinically significant incorrect lab test results, such as falsely high or falsely low results, depending on the test.” Biotin or collagen for hair loss? Collagen is another one of the buzzy ingredients linked to hair growth. Unfortunately, when it comes to comparing biotin and collagen , neither is a silver bullet when it comes to fixing hair loss. But biotin has been studied more thoroughly, which means it's a safer bet when it comes to seeing results. Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Viviscal Hair Growth Supplements $40 AMAZON Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Vegamour Biotin Gummies $36 $25 (31% off) AMAZON Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Women's Hair Growth Supplements $88 AMAZON Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Hers Biotin + Minoxidil Gummy $35 HERS The best biotin for hair The NIH suggests that adults should get about 30 micrograms of biotin per day (and women who are expecting or breastfeeding should consult a doctor about the proper amount to increase). Experts agree the best way to amp up your biotin intake (deficiency or not) is the old-fashioned way: through your diet. Here are some options along with how they can help you meet the daily allowance of biotin, per The Cleveland Clinic : Organ meats like liver: 3 ounces of chicken liver has the highest amount of biotin and can get you to 460% of the goal; 3 ounces of beef liver is 100% of your 30 mcg goal. Fish: Salmon has the highest amount of biotin, with 17% of your daily goal in 3 ounces. Meat: Pork chops and ground beef are both biotin-rich, both with 13% of your daily serving in 3 ounces, Eggs: One cooked egg has 33% of your daily biotin goal, which means the protein-rich breakfast of 3 eggs will help you fulfill it easily. Seeds and nuts: A fourth of a cup of either peanuts or sunflower seeds have 16% and 9% of your daily allotment of biotin, respectively. Vegetables: Focus on vegetables like canned mushrooms, sweet potatoes, spinach, and broccoli, all of which have just under 10% of your daily biotin goal. If you eat a well-rounded, healthy diet—one that includes healthy fats, lean proteins, whole grains, as well as a wide variety of fruits and veggies —you are probably consuming adequate levels of biotin already. However, if you want to consume an extra-effective hair-healthy diet , consider focusing on the following specific (and delicious) biotin sources : avocado, sweet potato, eggs, salmon, tuna, sardines, broccoli, spinach, almonds, mushrooms, legumes, walnuts, and sunflower seeds. Can biotin shampoo help hair growth? You may have noticed that biotin is also found in a ton of hair treatments , including shampoos, conditioners, and serums. However, there’s very little evidence to support the idea that applying biotin topically to hair can improve hair quality. “While appropriate consumption of biotin in diet leads to healthy hair, little data support the role of biotin in healthy individuals,” one scientific analysis states . “Many of the OTC hair loss treatments found in the best seller lists of the online retailers' websites were natural products with very few evidence about their efficacy,” it concludes. Hair treatments enriched with biotin That said, many biotin-enriched treatments include other high-quality, hair-healthy ingredients that can enhance fullness, volume, and shine—which, let’s admit, can be a savior during high hair-shed seasons. If you would like to try a shampoo, conditioner, or other hair product containing biotin, these are some worthy options to try: Save to wishlist Save to wishlist Coco & Eve Hair Volumising Shampoo & Conditioner Set $45 ΑΜΑΖΟΝ Coco & Eve’s Boost Therapy range is formulated to increase volume, strength, and thickness with a combination of biotin, hydrating panthenol, coconut oil, and the brand’s proprietary complex, Biovolume 128™, which is clinically proven to increase hair volume after three months. The recipe for success? Bamboo extract, biotin, and squalane.
